authoritySource: Dr. Ahmed Hesham - OET Reading Authoring Rulebook v1

sections.title: Part A rapid matching and short answers

rules.title: Exactly 42 Reading items
rules.body: Every authored Reading paper has exactly 42 raw-score items: Part A has 20, Part B has 6, and Part C has 16. The publish gate rejects any other shape.

rules.title: 30/42 equals 350/500
rules.body: Reading grading uses the canonical raw to scaled anchor 30/42 equals 350/500. Authors never hardcode pass/fail thresholds in extracted structures.

rules.title: Part A has four source texts
rules.body: Part A must contain four short clinical source texts and 20 items linked to those texts. Items may be matching, short answer, or sentence completion.

rules.title: Short answers remain exact-match safe
rules.body: Part A accepted synonyms must be clinically equivalent spelling or abbreviation variants only. Do not accept a medically different concept.

rules.title: Part B is six three-option MCQs
rules.body: Part B has six independent workplace extracts and exactly one three-option multiple-choice item per extract.

rules.title: Part C is two texts and sixteen four-option MCQs
rules.body: Part C has two longer texts with eight four-option multiple-choice questions per text. Correct answers must be A, B, C, or D.

rules.title: Answers must come from the source
rules.body: Every correct answer and explanation must be grounded in the supplied source text or answer key, never general medical knowledge.

rules.title: Distractors are plausible and cited
rules.body: Wrong options should be plausible misreadings of the text and explanations should identify why they are wrong without revealing unsupported facts.
